conflicting_values: '5.35/7GRv' (09/25 chart, printed in the level-5 row)
source_conflicts.reason: The 09/25 chart prints '7GRv' in the level-5 row, inconsistent with the numeric level prefix 5; the 07/23 chart prints 5GRv, consistent with the level. The '7' is almost certainly a typo in the 2025 PDF.

conflicting_values: '4.35/7GRv' (09/25 chart, printed in the level-4 row)
source_conflicts.reason: The 09/25 chart prints '7GRv' in the level-4 row, inconsistent with the numeric level prefix 4; the 07/23 chart prints 4GRv, consistent with the level. The '7' is almost certainly a typo in the 2025 PDF.

conflicting_values: '1 tube + 3 oz. Majicreme Developer' with '2oz tube' (09/25 chart and current product page)
'1.7 oz. tube + 2.6 oz. Majicreme Developer' (07/23 chart and how-to-use PDF)
source_conflicts.reason: The 2025 'Improved' Majirel relaunch increased the tube to 2 oz; the ratio (1:1.5) is identical in all sources. Newer official sources preferred.

conflicting_values: '1 tube (1.7 oz.) to 2.6 oz. Diactivateur developer; 6-vol. (1.8%) or 9-vol. (2.7%)' (Dia Light Color Chart 2025 PDF)
'1 tube (2 oz.) to 3 oz. DIActivateur Developer; 6-vol. (1.8%), 9-vol. (2.7%) or 15-vol. (4.5%); 6/9 vol recommended' (official Dia Light product page, accessed 2026-06)
source_conflicts.preferred_value: 1 tube (2 oz.) + 3 oz. DIActivateur Developer (1:1.5); 6-vol, 9-vol, or 15-vol, with 6/9-vol recommended
source_conflicts.reason: The product page describes the newer 2 oz tube format ('Larger tube size, now 2 oz. vs. 1.7 oz.') and is the more recently updated official source; ratio (1:1.5) and 2-20 minute time are identical in both.

issue_log: Per-shade swatch presence could not be verified because data was taken from PDF text extraction (pypdf); both charts are visual swatch charts but official_swatch_image_exists is left null rather than asserted.

PDF text extraction produced doubled text artifacts for some Dia Light shade names (e.g. 'Dazzling Dazzling Pearl Pearl MilkshakeMilkshake'); names were de-duplicated and the affected records carry an assumption note.
Majirel 09/25 chart prints '5.35/7GRv' and '4.35/7GRv'; treated as typos and logged in source_conflicts.

Majirel 07/23 chart describes the Majirouge sub-range ('Intense, vibrant, long-lasting reds & coppers', same 1:1.5 / 35 min guidelines), but extracted text does not explicitly label which shade codes belong to Majirouge; no shades were attributed to Majirouge to avoid guessing.
Majirel '901S/NB' and '900S/NN' appear on the 07/23 chart with ambiguous section placement; recorded as partially_confirmed without sub-range attribution.

Majirel Boosters (Blue, Violet, Green, Copper, Clear) on the 09/25 chart carry no shade codes; captured at line level rather than as coded shade records (except Clear, recorded from the 07/23 'MAJIREL CLEAR' section).

Majirel High Lift & Majiblond Ultra are documented in the how-to-use PDF and 07/23 chart at line level, but no individual High Lift shade codes (other than the ambiguous 900S/901S) were readable in any fetched source; High Lift shade list is a completeness gap.

Dia Light official product page (accessed June 2026) claims a '64 shade palette with 7 new shades'; only 59 coded shades plus an ambiguous 'Clear' were observable in the 2025 chart PDF, so up to ~4 shades may be missing from these records.
Dia Light chart PDF (LPJUNDL01) prints no revision date; the 2025 designation comes from the file name and hosting page.
saloncentric.com fallback was not needed because official us.lorealprofessionnel.com sources were accessible.
